opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

wattage.description: The total power output of the PSU in watts (e.g., 1000)

form_factor.description: The physical size and shape of the PSU (e.g., ATX)

efficiency_rating.description: The 80 PLUS efficiency certification level (e.g., 80 PLUS Gold)

cybernetics_efficiency_rating.description: The Cybernetics efficiency rating of the PSU (e.g., Cybenetics Titanium)

cybernetics_noise_rating.description: The Cybernetics noise rating of the PSU (e.g., Cybernetics A++)

modular.description: The modularity of the PSU cables (e.g., Full, Semi-Modular, Non-Modular)

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes the casing color

lighting.description: Lighting capabilities for this product. Use RGB for non-addressable RGB, ARGB for addressable RGB, Multicolor for non-RGB multi-color lighting, Fixed for a single fixed lighting color, None when the product has no lighting, Display for configurable displays, and Fixed Color Display for displays limited to fixed colors such as black and white.

length.description: The length of the PSU in mm (e.g., 140)

fanless.description: Whether the PSU operates without a fan

atx_24_pin.description: The number of ATX 24-pin (20+4) connectors (main motherboard power)

eps_8_pin.description: The number of EPS12V 8-pin (4+4) connectors (CPU power)

pcie_12vhpwr.description: The number of PCIe 5.0 12VHPWR (12+4) pin connectors (for high-end GPUs). Also referred to as 16-pin (12+4).

pcie_6_plus_2_pin.description: The number of PCIe 6+2-pin connectors (commonly used for GPUs)

sata.description: The number of SATA power connectors

molex_4_pin.description: The number of Molex 4-pin peripheral connectors (for older devices)

floppy_4_pin.description: The number of Floppy 4-pin connectors. Increasingly rare.

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description: Product series name The manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the PSU. Manufacturer, wattage (`wattage`), form factor (`form_factor`), modularity (`modular`), and efficiency rating (`efficiency_rating`) are stored separately, so do NOT repeat any of them in the series. Wattage is NEVER part of the series. Examples: 'Corsair RM1000x 1000W 80+ Gold' → series='RMx'. 'Seasonic Prime TX-1600 ATX 3.1' → series='Prime TX'. 'ASUS ROG Loki SFX-L 1000W Platinum' → series='ROG Loki SFX-L'.

variant.description: Specific variant in the series Most PSUs should have an empty variant. Wattage, efficiency rating, form factor, modularity, length, and color are all stored in their own fields. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series + wattage that needs its own selector — for example a marketed hardware revision (`2024` refresh, `R2`) or an ATX spec refresh sold next to the previous SKU. Examples: 'Corsair RM1000x' → variant=''. 'Corsair RM1000x 2024' → variant='2024'. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, wattage, manufacturer_color) group; if every PSU in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description: Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant. The manufacturer's marketed colorway / casing finish name, verbatim. Common values: 'Black', 'White', 'Black / White'. Branded special / collab colorways belong here when the rest of the SKU is identical (e.g., 'Hatsune Miku Edition'). Most stock PSUs are 'Black'. Do NOT put wattage, efficiency, modularity, form factor, or family / model words like 'RMx', 'Prime' in manufacturer_color — those belong in dedicated fields or the series.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
